entries.name: processual entity
entries.description: An occurrent [span:Occurrent] that exists in time by occurring or happening, has temporal parts and always involves and depends on some entity.

entries.name: uterine cervix
entries.description: Lower, narrow portion of the uterus where it joins with the top end of the vagina.

entries.name: naris
entries.description: Orifice of the olfactory system. The naris is the route by which odorants enter the olfactory system[MAH].

entries.name: nose
entries.description: The olfactory organ of vertebrates, consisting of nares, olfactory epithelia and the structures and skeletal framework of the nasal cavity.

entries.name: islet of Langerhans
entries.description: The clusters of hormone-producing cells that are scattered throughout the pancreas.

entries.name: pituitary gland
entries.description: The pituitary gland is an endocrine gland that secretes hormones that regulate many other glands [GO]. An endocrine gland located ventral to the diencephalon and derived from mixed neuroectodermal and non neuroectodermal origin [ZFIN].

entries.name: submucosa
entries.description: A layer of dense irregular connective tissue that lines organs and supports the mucosa, as well as joins the mucosa to the bulk of underlying smooth muscle. [WP,unvetted].

entries.name: peripheral nervous system
entries.description: A major division of the nervous system that contains nerves which connect the central nervous system (CNS) with sensory organs, other organs, muscles, blood vessels and glands.

entries.name: parasympathetic nervous system
entries.description: The parasympathetic nervous system is one of the two divisions of the vertebrate autonomic nervous system. Parasympathetic nerves emerge cranially as pre ganglionic fibers from oculomotor, facial, glossopharyngeal and vagus and from the sacral region of the spinal cord. Most neurons are cholinergic and responses are mediated by muscarinic receptors. The parasympathetic system innervates, for example: salivary glands, thoracic and abdominal viscera, bladder and genitalia[GO].
synonyms: parasympathetic part of autonomic division of nervous system
pars parasympathica divisionis autonomici systematis nervosi
pars parasympathica divisionis autonomici systematis nervosi

entries.name: somatic nervous system
entries.description: Part of peripheral nervous system that includes the somatic parts of the cranial and spinal nerves and their ganglia and the peripheral sensory receptors.
synonyms: somatic part of peripheral nervous system

entries.name: sympathetic nervous system
entries.description: The sympathetic nervous system is one of the two divisions of the vertebrate autonomic nervous system (the other being the parasympathetic nervous system). The sympathetic preganglionic neurons have their cell bodies in the thoracic and lumbar regions of the spinal cord and connect to the paravertebral chain of sympathetic ganglia. Innervate heart and blood vessels, sweat glands, viscera and the adrenal medulla. Most sympathetic neurons, but not all, use noradrenaline as a post-ganglionic neurotransmitter [GO].
synonyms: pars sympathica divisionis autonomici systematis nervosi
sympathetic part of autonomic division of nervous system
pars sympathica divisionis autonomici systematis nervosi

entries.name: zone of skin
entries.description: Any portion of the organ that covers that body and consists of a layer of epidermis and a layer of dermis.

entries.name: non-material anatomical boundary
entries.description: A non-material anatomical entity of two dimensions. Anatomical boundaries are contiguous structures.

entries.name: endocrine pancreas
entries.description: The part of the pancreas that is part of the endocrine system and is made up of islet cells, which produce insulin, glucagon and somatostatin.
synonyms: islets of Langerhans part of pancreas

entries.name: exocrine pancreas
entries.description: The part of the pancreas that is part of the exocrine system and which produces and store zymogens of digestive enzymes, such as chymotrypsinogen and trypsinogen in the acinar cells [GO].

entries.name: compound eye
entries.description: A light sensing organ composed of ommatidia.

entries.name: camera-type eye
entries.description: An organ of sight that includes the camera-type eyeball and supporting structures such as the lacrimal apparatus, the conjunctiva, the eyelid.

entries.name: sense organ
entries.description: An organ that is capable of transducing sensory stimulus to the nervous system.

entries.name: cutaneous appendage
entries.description: Anatomical projection that protrudes from the skin. Examples: hair, nail, feather, claw, hoof, horn, wattle, spur, beak, antler, bristle and some scales.

entries.name: feather
entries.description: One of the epidermal growths that form the distinctive outer covering, or plumage, on birds. Feathers are formed in tiny follicles in the epidermis, or outer skin layer, that produce keratin proteins.

entries.name: wing
entries.description: Appendage that is shaped in order to produce lift for flight through the air.

entries.name: forelimb wing
entries.description: Forelimb that is used to produce lift for flight through the air.

entries.name: tube
entries.description: Any hollow cylindrical anatomical structure containing a lumen through which substances are transported.

entries.name: appendage
entries.description: Major subdivision of an organism that protrudes from the body[DOS, CARO].

entries.name: lymph node
entries.description: Any of the rounded masses of lymphoid tissue that are surrounded by a capsule of connective tissue, are distributed along the lymphatic vessels, and contain numerous lymphocytes which filter the flow of lymph.

entries.name: lamina propria
entries.description: A thin layer of loose connective tissue which lies beneath the epithelium and together with the epithelium constitutes the mucosa[WP]. The lamina propria contains capillaries and a central lacteal (lymph vessel) in the small intestine, as well as lymphoid tissue. Lamina propria also contains glands with the ducts opening on to the mucosal epithelium, that secrete mucus and serous secretions.

entries.name: lamina propria of trachea
entries.description: A lamina propria that is part of a respiratory airway.

entries.name: obsolete cranial structure
entries.description: obsoleted as it was a grouping class that served no purpose

entries.name: head
entries.description: The head is the anterior-most division of the body [GO].

entries.name: primary ovarian follicle
entries.description: An ovarian follicle that has one layer of granulosa cells.

entries.name: secondary ovarian follicle
entries.description: A maturing ovarian follicle that has two more more layers of granulosa cells, up to the onset of antrum formation.

entries.name: tertiary ovarian follicle
entries.description: A follicle that has reached the most mature stage of folliculogenesis, characterized by the presence of the antrum.

entries.name: follicular fluid
entries.description: The fluid surrounding the ovum and granulosa cells in the ovarian follicle.

entries.name: follicular antrum
entries.description: Region of ovarian follicle filled with follicular fluid.

entries.name: Leydig's organ
entries.description: A hemopoietic organ found in elasmobranchs. Along with the spleen and special tissue around the gonads, this structure produces red blood cells and it is nestled along the top and bottom of the esophagus.

entries.name: odontode scale
entries.description: Multi-tissue structure composed of enameloid, dentine surrounding a pulp cavity, and bone of attachment anchoring the element into the dermis[VSAO].

entries.name: serous membrane
entries.description: Multi-tissue structure that is comprised of a secretory epithelial layer (mesothelium) and a connective tissue layer.

entries.name: tendon
entries.description: Dense regular connective tissue that connects muscle to bone[VSAO].

entries.name: dorsal root ganglion
entries.description: Sensory ganglia located on the dorsal spinal roots within the vertebral column. The spinal ganglion cells are pseudounipolar. The single primary branch bifurcates sending a peripheral process to carry sensory information from the periphery and a central branch which relays that information to the spinal cord or brain. (MSH) * ganglion found on the posterior root of each spinal nerve, composed of the unipolar nerve cell bodies of the sensory neurons of the nerve. (CSP).
synonyms: spinal ganglion part of peripheral nervous system

entries.name: ganglion
entries.description: A biological tissue mass, most commonly a mass of nerve cell bodies.

entries.name: stemma
entries.description: Eye-spots which may be set into a pit to reduce the angles of light that enters and affects the eyespot, to allow the organism to deduce the angle of incoming light.

entries.name: simple eye
entries.description: An eye with one concave chamber. Note that 'simple' does not imply a reduced level of complexity or acuity.

entries.name: pinhole eye
entries.description: Simple eye that has a small aperture (which may be adjustable) and deep pit. It is only found in the nautiloids.

entries.name: spherical lensed eye
entries.description: Simple eye that consists of a lens of one refractive index.

entries.name: simple eye with multiple lenses
entries.description: Simple eye that has two or more lenses. Some marine organisms bear more than one lens; for instance the copeopod Pontella has three. The outer has a parabolic surface, countering the effects of spherical aberration while allowing a sharp image to be formed. Copilla's eyes have two lenses, which move in and out like a telescope.

entries.name: fornix of vagina
entries.description: The deepest portions of the vagina, extending into the recesses created by the vaginal portion of cervix[WP,unvetted].

entries.name: fornix of brain
entries.description: A C-shaped bundle of fibres (axons) in the brain, and carries signals from the hippocampus to the mammillary bodies and septal nuclei. It is typically divided into the columns (crus), body, commissure and the pre-commissural and post-commissural fornix (MM).

entries.name: macula lutea
entries.description: An oval area in the retina, usually located temporal to the posterior pole of the eye and slightly below the level of the optic disk. It is characterized by the presence of a yellow pigment diffusely permeating the inner layers, contains the fovea centralis in its center, and provides the best phototopic visual acuity. It is devoid of retinal blood vessels, except in its periphery, and receives nourishment from the choriocapillaris of the choroid. (From Cline et al., Dictionary of Visual Science, 4th ed).

entries.name: macula
entries.description: Thickened areas of the saccule or utricle where the termination of the vestibular nerve occurs[MESH,modified].

entries.name: vessel
entries.description: A tubular structure that contains, conveys body fluid, such as blood or lymph.

entries.name: ureter
entries.description: Muscular duct that propels urine from the kidneys to the urinary bladder, or related organs.

entries.name: urethra
entries.description: The fibromuscular tubular canal through which urine is discharged from the bladder to the exterior via the external urinary meatus; in males, the urethra is joined by the ejaculatory ducts and serves as a passageway for semen during ejaculation, as well as a canal for urine during voiding; in females, the urethra is shorter and emerges above the vaginal opening.

entries.name: duct
entries.description: A tubular structure that transports secreted or excreted substances.

entries.name: large intestine
entries.description: A subdivision of the digestive tract that connects the small intestine to the cloaca or anus. Lacks or has few villi[Kardong].

entries.name: anatomical wall
entries.description: Organ component adjacent to an organ cavity and which consists of a maximal aggregate of organ component layers.

entries.name: anatomical structure
entries.description: Material anatomical entity that is a single connected structure with inherent 3D shape generated by coordinated expression of the organism's own genome.

entries.name: organ
entries.description: Anatomical structure that performs a specific function or group of functions [WP].

entries.name: organ subunit
entries.description: A part of an organ that constitutes a distinct modular sub-unit. In some cases, the organ may also contain other sub-units of identical or similar types, in other cases this may be a distinct entity.
synonyms: organ region with fixed fiat boundary

entries.name: organ part
entries.description: A multicellular structure that is a part of an organ.

entries.name: respiratory tract
entries.description: Anatomical structure that is part of the respiratory system. In mammals consists of upper and lower tracts.

entries.name: fully formed stage
entries.description: The stage of development at which the animal is fully formed, including immaturity and maturity. Includes both sexually immature stage, and adult stage.

entries.name: obsolete embryo stage part
entries.description: A stage that is part of the embryo stage.

entries.name: embryo stage
entries.description: A life cycle stage that starts with fertilization and ends with the fully formed embryo.

entries.name: larval stage
entries.description: A distinct juvenile stage many animals undergo before metamorphosis into adults. Animals with indirect development such as insects, amphibians, or cnidarians typically have a larval phase of their life cycle.

entries.name: pupal stage
entries.description: A life cycle stage of holometabolous insects in which the organism is a pupa and starts with the larval-pupal apolysis and ends with pupal-adult apolysis.

entries.name: death stage
entries.description: End of the life of an organism.

entries.name: proximo-distal subdivision of respiratory tract
entries.description: An section of a respiratory tract.

entries.name: regional part of nervous system
entries.description: Any part or collection of parts of the central or peripheral nervous system. Parts may span both CNS and PNS.

entries.name: renal glomerulus
entries.description: A capillary tuft which forms a close network with the visceral epithelium (podocytes) and the mesangium to form the filtration barrier and is surrounded by Bowman's capsule in nephrons of the vertebrate kidney[GO].

entries.name: subdivision of skeletal system
entries.description: Anatomical cluster consisting of the skeletal elements and articular elements that are part of an individual subdivision of the organism.

entries.name: external ectoderm
entries.description: The surface (external) layer of ectoderm which begins to proliferate shortly after separation from the neuroectoderm.

entries.name: mixed endoderm/mesoderm-derived structure
entries.description: An anatomical structure that develops from the endoderm and the mesoderm.

entries.name: mixed ectoderm/mesoderm/endoderm-derived structure
entries.description: An anatomical structure that develops from the ectoderm, mesoderm and endoderm.

entries.name: male reproductive system
entries.description: The organs associated with producing offspring in the gender that produces spermatozoa.

entries.name: mesonephros
entries.description: The second stage of the kidney. It serves as the main excretory organ of aquatic vertebrates and as a temporary embryonic kidney in higher vertebrates. It is composed of the mesonephric duct (also called the Wolffian duct), mesonephric tubules, and associated capillary tufts. A single tubule and its associated capillary tuft is called a mesonephric excretory unit; these units are similar in structure and function to nephrons of the adult kidney. The mesonephros is derived from intermediate mesoderm in the vertebrate embryo.

entries.name: metanephros
entries.description: In mammals, the metanephros is the excretory organ of the fetus, which develops into the mature kidney and is formed from the rear portion of the nephrogenic cord. The metanephros is an endocrine and metabolic organ that filters the blood and excretes the end products of body metabolism in the form of urine[GO].

entries.name: adult mammalian kidney
entries.description: A kidney at the most advanced stage of development in a mammal.

entries.name: mesonephric tubule
entries.description: A mesonephric tubule is an epithelial tube that is part of the mesonephros[GO]. Genital ridge that is next to the mesonephros[WP].

entries.name: ureteric bud
entries.description: An epithelial swelling on the Wolffian duct that elongates to invade the adjacent metanephric mesenchyme[MP].

entries.name: morula
entries.description: A spherical embryonic mass of blastomeres formed before the blastula and resulting from cleavage of the fertilized ovum.

entries.name: zona pellucida
entries.description: A glycoprotein membrane surrounding the plasma membrane of an oocyte. It is a vital constitutive part of the latter, external but not extraneous to it. The zona pellucida first appears in multilaminar primary oocytes.

entries.name: inner cell mass
entries.description: A mass of cells that develop into the body of the embryo and some extraembryonic tissues.
synonyms: embryoblastus; massa cellularis interna; pluriblastus senior

entries.name: trophoblast
entries.description: The mesectodermal cell layer arising from the trophectoderm that erodes the uterine mucosa and contributes to the formation of the placenta.

entries.name: hypoblast (generic)
entries.description: Missing definition for term UBERON:0000089. Please replace it using linked reference.

entries.name: blastocele
entries.description: Fluid-filled central region of a blastocyst.

entries.name: bilaminar disc
entries.description: A flattened, almost circular bilaminar plate of cells formed when the inner cell mass (aka embryoblast) forms two epithelial layers, each of a distinct lineage, separated by an extracellular basement membrane: the external (dorsal) layer is called the epiblast and the internal (ventral) layer is called the hypoblast (aka primitive endoderm); together, they compose the bilaminar embryonic disc.

entries.name: post-embryonic stage
entries.description: Stage succeeding embryo, including mature structure.

entries.name: sulcus
entries.description: A depression or fissure in the surface of an organ.

entries.name: membrane organ
entries.description: Nonparenchymatous organ that primarily consists of dense connective tissue organized into a sheet which interconnects two or more organs, separates two or more body spaces from one another, or surrounds an organ or body part. Examples: interosseous membrane of forearm, obturator membrane, tympanic membrane, fibrous pericardium, fascia lata, dura mater. [FMA].

entries.name: cardiac neural crest
entries.description: Portion of neural crest that develops from the dorsal neural tube. It overlaps the vagal neural crest and migrates to populate the pharyngeal arches 3, 4 and 6 (producing structures in the head) and to the heart, forming connective tissue that separates the great vessels of the heart. [Wikipedia].

entries.name: blastopore
entries.description: The orifice of invagination of the endomesodermal cells during the embryonic stages of an organism.

entries.name: lobe of lung
entries.description: A lung lobe is one of the rounded projections that compose the lung[GO].

entries.name: lung vasculature
entries.description: The lung vasculature is composed of the tubule structures that carry blood or lymph in the lungs[GO].

entries.name: obsolete life cycle stage boundary
entries.description: Instant starting or ending a life cycle stage. These can be arbitrary/fiat.

entries.name: life cycle
entries.description: An entire span of an organism's life. In metazoans, commences with the zygote stage and ends with the death of the organism.

entries.name: life cycle stage
entries.description: A spatiotemporal region encompassing some part of the life cycle of an organism.

entries.name: zygote stage
entries.description: A stage at which the organism is a single cell produced by means of sexual reproduction.

entries.name: cleavage stage
entries.description: The first few specialized divisions of an activated animal egg; Stage consisting of division of cells in the early embryo. The zygotes of many species undergo rapid cell cycles with no significant growth, producing a cluster of cells the same size as the original zygote. The different cells derived from cleavage are called blastomeres and form a compact mass called the morula. Cleavage ends with the formation of the blastula.

entries.name: blastula stage
entries.description: An early stage of embryonic development in animals. It is produced by cleavage of a fertilized ovum and consists of a spherical layer of around 128 cells surrounding a central fluid-filled cavity called the blastocoel. The blastula follows the morula and precedes the gastrula in the developmental sequence.
